Flagging as a scoping gap in the brief, not an implementer defect.

## Verdict 1 — Spec compliance: PASS

The diff matches the brief verbatim on every file/step: `testimonialSection.html` track/dots wrapper, SCSS margin change and `&_track`/`&_dots`/`&_dot` block (character-for-character match to the brief), `testimonial-carousel.js` (byte-identical to the brief's Step 3), both Slick calls removed from `script.js`, `head.html`'s Slick `<link>` block removed, `footer.html`'s Slick script tag removed and the testimonial-carousel resource tag inserted exactly where specified (immediately after `navbar.js`, before `form-handler.js`), and the entire `plugins/slick/` directory deleted (confirmed via diffstat: pure deletions, no additions).

The two additional fixes are legitimate, correctly scoped, non-scope-creep corrections:
- **`flex-shrink: 0` on `&_item`**: Necessary — with the brief's flexbox layout, default `flex-shrink: 1` + `min-width: auto` lets the browser shrink items unevenly based on content when combined flex-basis (300% at 1-item/view) exceeds the container, which the report measured concretely (194/268/169px instead of uniform slides, causing real page overflow). This is a real bug independent of any implementer misreading — Slick previously handled width/positioning via inline styles set by its own JS, not CSS flexbox, so this gap wasn't present in the old implementation and had no analogue to catch in review.
- **`overflow: hidden` on `&__slider`**: Necessary — `translateX` doesn't remove off-screen slides from document flow, so without clipping they'd cause page overflow. Slick's own CSS (`slick-list { overflow: hidden }`, now deleted along with the plugin) provided this previously; the brief's hand-rolled replacement needed an equivalent and didn't have one.

Both are scoped purely to the new track/dots rules and the `&_item` selector; they don't touch ratings, author byline, card background/border-radius/padding, or any other existing rule (confirmed by reading the full compiled `_testimonial-section.scss` — only the two flagged lines and the `&_track`/`&_dots`/`&_dot` block are new/changed relative to `&_item`'s other properties).

## Verdict 2 — Code quality: **Approved**

- `script.js` remains syntactically valid after removing both Slick blocks (`node --check` passes); the `$(document).ready(...)` wrapper and Modal Popup / blob animation code are untouched and correctly re-closed.
- `testimonial-carousel.js` passes `node --check`.
- Dots/track markup matches the JS's selectors exactly: `.testimonial__slider_track`, `.testimonial__slider_dots`, `.testimonial__slider_dot`, `.is-active` all line up between template, JS, and SCSS.
- `getComputedStyle(track).columnGap` is read live (not hardcoded), correctly handling both the 992px slide-count breakpoint and the separate 767px `@include tablet` gap-zeroing breakpoint, per the plan's global constraint.
- `hugo --quiet` builds cleanly; `public/plugins/slick/` is absent post-build.
- Minor non-blocking finding: `_service-section.scss` still has orphaned `.slick-*` selectors (dead CSS for the disabled `service__slider` section) — outside this task's brief scope but worth a follow-up ticket if "retire Slick entirely" is meant literally.

Files reviewed: `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/.superpowers/sdd/task-3-brief.md`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/.superpowers/sdd/task-3-report.md`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/.superpowers/sdd/review-task3-focused.diff`, plus live checks against `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/assets/scss/components/_testimonial-section.scss`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/assets/scss/components/_service-section.scss`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/layouts/partials/testimonialSection.html`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/assets/js/script.js`, `/Users/rod/build/begbie-hugo/themes/portio/assets/js/testimonial-carousel.js`.</result>

## Final whole-branch review: `chore/js-vanilla-rewrite-phase2`

**Ready to merge: YES** — with one optional Minor cleanup recommended (non-blocking).

### Verification performed (independent, not a rubber-stamp)

- **Fresh build:** `rm -rf public && hugo --quiet` succeeds on Hugo 0.163.3. Output `public/js/` contains exactly the four expected modules (`navbar.min.js`, `testimonial-carousel.min.js`, `video-popup.min.js`, `form-handler.min.js`); no plugin JS/CSS ships.
- **`public/plugins/` is empty** (`find` returns nothing under it). See informational note below on why the directory node exists at all.
- **Reference sweep** across all live `layouts/`, `assets/js/`, `assets/scss/`, `config.toml`, `data/` (excluding the vendored `bootstrap-4.5.2/` source): **zero** live references to jQuery, Magnific/Magnafic, TweenMax/TimelineMax/GSAP, Masonry, imagesLoaded, Waypoint, headroom, `bootstrap.min.js`, `initialize(`, `google.maps`, `#map`, `params.map`, or the deleted `script.js`. The only textual hits for "Magnific" are two explanatory code comments in `video-popup.js`. The only "slick" hits are the dead CSS below.
- **Template wiring confirmed:** `navbar.html` toggler (`[data-toggle="collapse"]`, `data-target="#navbarCollapse"`, target `.collapse.navbar-collapse`, nav links `.navbar-nav>li>a`) all match `navbar.js`'s selectors. `testimonialSection.html` now emits `_track` + empty `_dots`, matching `testimonial-carousel.js`.

### Cross-boundary findings (things a single-task review wouldn't catch)

- **Module style is fully consistent** across all four — including the unchanged `form-handler.js`. All use `window.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", …)`, `var`, named function declarations, 2-space indent. Note the design doc said "IIFE-wrapped"; the implementer instead matched the *existing* `form-handler.js` DOMContentLoaded pattern. That's the better consistency target, so I regard the deviation as correct, not a defect.
- **`navbar.js` exceeds the design sketch — correctly.** The design described a naive "toggle a `show` class and let CSS animate." That alone would *not* animate: Bootstrap's `.collapsing` sets `height:0` and needs an explicit pixel height set/cleared per transition. The implementer ported Bootstrap's real height-animation (collapsing class + reflow + timed cleanup). This is a faithful, working reimplementation, not scope creep.
- **`footer.html` script order** (navbar → testimonial → video-popup → form-handler, no jQuery) is sensible: the four modules are mutually independent with no shared globals, so order is immaterial, and they're at end-of-body so DOMContentLoaded wrapping is safe.
- No selector/class collisions between the new `.video-popup*`, `.testimonial__slider_*`, and existing styles. `contact/list.html` map column correctly widened `col-lg-6` → `col-lg-12` after the `#map` removal.

### Findings ranked

**Critical / Important:** none.

**Minor (1) — orphaned Slick CSS in `themes/portio/assets/scss/components/_service-section.scss`** (lines ~62–108: `.slick-list`, `.slick-dots`, `.slick-active`, `.slick-slide`). This is the Task-3 carry-over. It is genuinely inert: `data/serviceSection.yml` has `enable: false`, the service section never renders, and nothing now emits `.slick-*` classes anyway. It *does* still `@import` into the compiled CSS bundle, so it adds a few hundred bytes of dead rules — mildly at odds with a performance phase whose stated goal was retiring Slick "entirely."

**My recommendation:** delete that block in a small follow-up commit on this branch before merge. It's cheap, zero-risk (dead + disabled section), and makes "Slick fully retired" literally true rather than nominally true. That said, it is *not* a merge blocker — if you'd rather keep the branch scoped exactly as the five task reviews approved, leaving it is acceptable and it can be swept up in Phase 3's asset-pipeline pass. Either choice is defensible; I lean toward the quick delete now.

### Informational (not a finding)

`themes/portio/static/plugins/` still exists on disk as an **empty directory** — that's why Hugo mkdir's an empty `public/plugins/`. Git cannot track empty directories, so it isn't committed and a fresh clone / Cloudflare build won't have it; it's purely a local-filesystem remnant of the file deletions on this machine. No action needed, but a `rmdir` locally would stop the empty `public/plugins/` from appearing in your dev builds.

The pre-existing hidden resume "experience" tab and the Apple Pay `.well-known` payload were correctly left untouched, per the design's non-goals.</result>
